title: The people behind the papers - Jonas Malkmus, Rushikesh Sheth and Rolf Zeller.
abstract: Establishing the digit-interdigit patterning in the developing limb bud requires complex signalling inputs. In a new paper in Development, Malkmus et al. use WNT inhibition and a combination of image-based and molecular tools to uncover the interplay between different signalling pathways in the establishment of digital patterning. Their experimental evidence supports the involvement of a Turing-type mechanism - a diffusion-based system involved in producing uniform patterns. We spoke to first author Jonas Malkmus and co-corresponding authors Rolf Zeller, Emeritus Professor at the University of Basel, Switzerland, and former group leader of the Developmental Genetics group, and Rushi Sheth, Senior Scientist in the lab and project lead, to find out more.
